title: Solanum esenbeckii
intro: Solanum esenbeckii, commonly known as the potato tree or potato bush, is a perennial plant native to South America that belongs to the Solanaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and promoting antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Solanum esenbeckii include solanine, solanidine, and glycoalkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Solanum esenbeckii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health ailments and promote overall well-being.

This family consists of a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the nightshade family. The classification of Solanum esenbeckii reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Solanaceae family.
